program: Travel English -- Europe Without a Translator
hub_subtitle: Travel English for the airport, the check-in counter, the hotel and passport control — so that Europe needs no translator

lesson.menu_title: The Most Beautiful Thing in the Market -- Rosângela in Lisbon
lesson.menu_desc: Superlatives at the market stall, asking the price, saying no politely, and paying -- alone -- 35 slides
lesson.subtitle: Lesson 7 -- Shopping and Souvenirs
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Rosângela | Lesson 7 -- Shopping and Souvenirs

listenings.text: Good morning, and welcome to the Feira da Ladra, the oldest market in Lisbon. Please listen to this information before you start. First, the days. This market is open on Tuesday and on Saturday only, from nine in the morning until six in the evening. Saturday is the busiest day, and it is also the most beautiful, because every stall is open. If you do not like crowds, come on Tuesday morning. Second, money. Most of the stalls here take cash only. There is one machine for money at the entrance, next to the church, and the line there is long on Saturday, so bring your cash from the hotel. The big shops in the street behind the market take cards, but the stalls, no. Third, the price. Many things here have no price tag. That is normal in this market: you look, you ask, and the seller tells you the price. And yes, you can bargain. It is not rude here. Ask for a small discount, and ask with a smile. But please, do not bargain on the food, and do not bargain for one euro. Fourth, and this is the most important thing on this recording: you can look at everything, and you can buy nothing. Nobody here is angry with that. Just say, no, thank you, I am just looking, and walk on. Last, your receipt. Ask for it, and keep it. Without the receipt you cannot change anything. Thank you very much, and enjoy the oldest market in the city.

Hello. My name is Cristina, and this is my stall. I am here every Saturday, for nineteen years. Can I tell you something? Many tourists are afraid of me. It is true! They see my stall, they look at my scarves for two seconds, and then they walk very fast, and they look at the ground. And I always think, oh, please, do not be afraid. I am not a lion. I am a woman with scarves. Look, I am going to tell you the secret of this market, and it is very short. You can look at everything, and you can buy nothing. That is not a problem for me. It is my job! People look all day, and ten people buy. That is a normal Saturday. So when you do not want it, please say it. Say, no, thank you, I am just looking. Say it in English, say it with a smile, and I am going to smile back at you, and that is the end. Nobody is angry. Nobody is offended. The only thing I do not like is when a person cannot say no, and buys my most expensive scarf, and goes home unhappy. That is terrible. I do not want your money like that. And one more thing. The most beautiful scarf on my stall is not the most expensive one. It never is. The best thing here is the one you love, and it costs twenty-two euros. So take your time. Look at everything. And ask me the price: that is what I am here for.

items.situation: You stop at a market stall. The seller comes to you immediately, and you do not want to buy anything. Say it politely, and do not walk away in silence.
tips: No, thank you. I am just looking.
Thank you, but not today. I am just browsing.

items.situation: There are three scarves in front of you. One is silk and it is forty-five euros. Say which one you like, and use a superlative.
tips: In my opinion, the silk one is the most beautiful.
This is the nicest scarf on the stall.

items.situation: There is no price tag on the blue tile. Ask.
tips: Excuse me. How much is this one, please?
There is no price tag. Could you tell me the price?

items.situation: You want two of them, and you want a better price. Ask, politely.
tips: If I take two, is this the best price you can offer?
Could you give me a discount for two, please?

items.situation: You want to try the jacket on before you buy it. Ask the seller.
tips: Can I try this on, please?

items.situation: You have no cash, only your card. Ask before the seller wraps it.
tips: I only have my card. Do you take cards here?

items.situation: It is a gift for your son, and you want the paper and the receipt. Ask for both.
tips: Could you wrap it, please? It is a gift.
And could I have the receipt, please?

items.situation: It is beautiful, but it is one hundred and ninety euros. Say no, and say why, without saying anything rude.
tips: It is the most beautiful thing here, but it is too expensive for me.
In my opinion it is lovely, but not today. Thank you very much.
